marker.name: Core Features · Tools ready when you need them
marker.purpose: Show that Modalia AI is an AI agent you pick the right tool from — not a feature list

heading:
The right tool,
exactly when you need it

psy.msg: Read test results and session notes together
psy.when: When you need to interpret assessment data in the context of a session
psy.desc: Modalia AI reviews assessment results alongside session notes, then surfaces cues and follow-up questions for the counselor to examine.
psy.painTag: When psych assessment interpretation feels stuck

mock.clientSub: Male · 42 · 8 sessions on record · Last assessed 2026-06-01
mock.userMsg: Please interpret this MMPI and temperament assessment together with the session notes.
mock.aiText1: Combining the assessment results (MMPI · temperament) with session notes, the core themes converge on 'perfectionist control' and 'defensive isolation'.
mock.scaleHarmDetail: Heightened sensitivity to potential threats, compulsive perseverance once a task is started — a pattern of harsh self-criticism to keep going.
mock.scaleSelfDetail: Diminished sense of purpose and self-acceptance, consistent with a state close to burnout.
mock.scaleSocialDetail: A cynical stance toward others, with a conspicuous pattern of self-isolation in relationships.
mock.aiText2: These traits align with the client's in-session complaints of 'absence of emotional connection within the family' and 'disconnection patterns.' The high persistence and extreme independence are plausibly interpreted as 'defensive isolation' chosen to avoid being hurt.
mock.flagBody: Surface depressive indicators fall within normal range; however, given the defensive response style and reactions to crisis-related items, a follow-up risk assessment and safety planning check are recommended in the next session.
mock.disclaimer: Interpretations provided by Modalia AI are for reference only. Final interpretation must be performed by a qualified clinician.

trx.msg: Turn your recording into a session record
trx.when: When re-listening and dictating feels like too much
trx.desc: Modalia AI separates and organizes what the counselor and client said, creating a foundation for your session notes.

mock.line1c: Welcome, Mr. Hong. How have you been this past week? It's getting quite chilly — was the commute alright?
mock.line1a: Oh, just so-so. My body's been aching more in the cold, and I had to rush because I couldn't get a cab.
mock.line2c: I see. Your body was already uncomfortable, and then the cab situation added extra stress. Would you like a warm drink?
mock.line2a: After our first session I felt a kind of relief, but I also felt embarrassed — like I'd said too much. My mind was a bit unsettled. Sleep is still hard.
mock.line3c: So relief and embarrassment came up at the same time. Has the sleep difficulty been ongoing since the first session?
mock.line3a: Yes. When I lie down I keep replaying what I said, so I just end up on my phone until early morning. I find myself wanting to push back our next session.

note.msg: Organize notes in the format you need — right away
note.when: When you're rewriting the same record into yet another form
note.desc: Using session notes and recordings, Modalia AI drafts SOAP, DAP, Family Center notes, and agency-submission records — each adapted to its purpose.
note.painTag: When reformatting for every template feels tedious

mock.sec1Body: Alleviating psychological distress from parentification in the family of origin and family relational dynamics
mock.sec2Body: Insight into parentification patterns; re-establishing personal boundaries within family relationships
mock.sec3Body: The client has repeatedly assumed a mediator role within the family and experiences excessive emotional responsibility during parental conflict.
mock.sec4Body: Parent-child boundaries in the family of origin are diffuse, with the client absorbing the parents' emotional burden. Genogram analysis revealed enmeshment with the mother and emotional distance from the father as central themes.
mock.sec4Mark: Genogram analysis revealed: enmeshment with mother and emotional distance from father
mock.sec5Body: Self-talk exercises helped the client differentiate family members' emotions from their own; intervention focused on redefining roles within the family.
mock.sec6Body: Continue psychological-distance maintenance exercises, review self-talk task completion, and elaborate boundary-setting scenarios within the family.
mock.sec7Body: When discussing distance from parents, resentment and guilt emerged together; a tendency to take personal responsibility for family role burdens was observed.
mock.sec8Body: In conflict situations, avoidance and silence recur instead of direct expression, after which emotional burden concentrates on the client.
mock.sec9Body: When the client spoke about their own needs, their voice became quieter and they avoided eye contact — suggesting a need to practice self-expression in a safe context.
mock.sec10Body: Emotional burden from the family-of-origin relationship and difficulty setting boundaries are identified as the primary counseling themes; adjusting intra-family communication patterns is recommended.

cnc.msg: Weave scattered clues into a coherent session arc
cnc.when: When you can see the repeating pattern but organizing it feels overwhelming
cnc.desc: Modalia AI organizes recurring emotions, relationships, and thought patterns into a hypothesis draft for the counselor to review.
cnc.painTag: When you're stuck on how to conceptualize the case

mock.overviewBody: Parentification in the family of origin and self-concept incongruence appear to be the core mechanisms sustaining the current conflict.
mock.bgBody: Youngest of three children; has served as emotional regulator within the family since childhood. Recently reports relational withdrawal following attempts at self-expression…
mock.cncBody: The incongruence between the 'fixer' self-concept and personal desires is expressed through repeated caretaker roles, making self-acceptance difficult…
mock.maintainBody: Family system expectations, guilt arising after boundary-setting, and self-worth derived from the caretaker role reinforce the pattern…
mock.citeBody: The gap between the 'protector' self-concept and the survival need of 'I need to live too' can be understood as a state in which self-acceptance has become difficult.
mock.citePoint1: Restoring awareness of organismic experience is the starting point for change
mock.citePoint2: Work to expand the self-concept beyond the caretaker role is needed
mock.evidenceBody: Session 3 — 'Being told I'm allowed to feel resentful — I kept repeating that phrase to myself.' Interpreted as the first signal of self-acceptance…
mock.emotionBody: Resentment, deflation, and self-blame appear repeatedly; difficulty expressing personal needs within relationships is observed.
mock.focusBody: Empathic reflection and self-acceptance work are prioritized to help the client safely recognize and express their emotions and needs.
mock.nextQBody: When 'feeling resentful' recurs, it may be worth exploring what desire the client most wanted to protect.

geno.msg: Map family relationships at a glance
geno.when: When you need a quick visual of family composition and relational patterns
geno.desc: Modalia AI uses family member information, relational cues, and conflict patterns to help organize a genogram and draft interpretive notes.
geno.painTag: When family relationships are tangled and complex

mock.insightLabel: Key relational axis · David Bennett ↔ Susan Hayes (conflict)
